rulings.Case Title: DGP ASSOCIATES LP VS. AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant American Guarantee And Liability Insurance Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Defendant American Guarantee And Liabiltiy Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 4, Defendant American Guarantee And Liability Insurance Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Defendant American Guarantee And Liabiltiy Insurance Company. The court grants defendant's motion for summary judgment. The court grants as to issue 1 [building damage]. Defendant's motion shows that Columbus Foods, LLC ("LLC") purchased the insurance and it was listed as the first named insured. LLC thus facially appears to be entitled to coverage and defendant properly paid sums to it. In opposition, plaintiff argues that LLC had no insurable interest, but fails to present evidence demonstrating that fact. As an aside, defendant's opposition to plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication shows that LLC did in fact have an insurable interest within the meaning of Cal. Food Service Corp. v. Great American Ins. Co. (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 892, 897. (See Ferrif Dec.). The court grants as to issue 2 [demolition]. Mr. Boll worked for Stellar and estimated the replacement cost of the building. At 90:12-16 of the Boll Depo, he indicates that the demolition costs were included in the replacement estimate. The court grants as to issue 3 [professional fees]. Plaintiff admits that plaintiff and LLC jointly retained Dempsey Partners to help present their claims. (Defendant's UMF 15). The undisputed evidence shows that there is a $100,000 limit for such professional fees and defendant paid that sum to LLC. The court grants as to issues 4 and 5 [causes of action] based on the above. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition, To Regulate Conduct At The Deposition, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Deposition, To Regulate Conduct At The Deposition, And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Defendant PG&E seeks to take an additional deposition of Plaintiff due to documents produced following the completion of three prior days of deposition. The motion is granted in part as follows: 1. The time allotted for the deposition is three hours. 2. The scope of deposition is limited to issues or matters contained in the 125 pages of documents produced by plaintiff on July 1, 2014 which documents were not previously available to defendant. 3. Counsel are ordered to state the nature of each applicable objection to a question in 15 words or less such as "calls for speculation", "lacks foundation", "inadmissible hearsay" or "I instruct the witness not to answer on the basis of attorney client privilege". 4. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAYNA BRAND VS. CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANTS CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN, DAVID COHN's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: "[A] trial court may order a terminating sanction for discovery abuse 'after considering the totality of the circumstances: [the] conduct of the party to determine if the actions were willful; the detriment to the propounding party; and the number of formal and informal attempts to obtain the discovery.' [Citation.] Generally, '[a] decision to order terminating sanctions should not be made lightly." Los Defensores, Inc. v. Gomez (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 377, 390. The Court finds that Plaintiff Rayna Brand has failed to comply with this Court's order of December 22, 2014 requiring her to 1) serve written verified answers and responses (without objection other than privilege) to moving parties' Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents; and 2) to pay monetary sanctions within 30 days. (Continued to Part 2 of 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAYNA BRAND VS. CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 3, Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery. (Duplicate entry - for tentative ruling only.) (Part 2 of 2.) Pursuant to CCP 2030.300(e) and 2031.300(c), the Court GRANTS the motion in part as follows: Within 10 days of the date of service of this order on Plaintiff, Plaintiff Rayna Brand shall comply fully with the December 22, 2014 Order. The Court advises Plaintiff Rayna Brand that if she fails to comply with this Order (i.e. does not provide Defendants' counsel with written verified answers and responses to the Form Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents and does pay the monetary sanctions), her lawsuit against Defendants may be dismissed due to her repeated failure to participate in discovery. Defendants' motion for a terminating sanction is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE and may be renewed if Plaintiff fails to comply with this order. Defendants' request for $520 in monetary sanctions is DENIED for failure to state in the motion the basis for the calculation of the requested amount of sanctions. See CCP 2023.040 (requiring declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL VS. HENRY KARNILOWICZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT HENRY KARNILOWICZ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The matter is continued on the court's motion to March 19, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EBONY ISLER VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, A PUBLIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend to clarify the statutory basis for the claim, e.g., Gov't 815.2. Plaintiff also has leave to more clearly allege ultimate facts regarding why the joint conference was negligent in this particular instance. The court rejects defendant's reliance on the governmental immunities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX REL. DENNIS VS. LILAY, INC D/B/A INSTANT TAX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Interrogatories From Lilay, Inc. And Kebron Daniel And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX REL. DENNIS HERRERA's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Interrogatories From Lilay, Inc. And Kebron Daniel And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Hearing Required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY ZABIT VS. NETFORTRIS ACQUISITION CO., INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 18, 2015, Line 14, Order To Show Cause Re Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction. The matter is taken off calendar. The parties stipulated to the relief sought. =(302/EHG)
